title: Twice Denied
prompt: A dragon guards the hatch. Rewrite the negations away to earn the key.
lore: Grooves worn into the stone, as if others came this way before you — each of them, it seems, alone.
help: This room is about rewriting away double and triple negations. `forward(B, lhs, rhs, eq, motive)` replaces `lhs` with `rhs`, the motive being the goal with `_` at the spot; rewriting the innermost negation first is easiest. Shortcut: `by rewrite(neg_tt)?;` / `by rewrite(neg_ff)?;` infers the arguments and suggests the full `by forward(…) then …;` step. Finish all 3 lemmas. (1) dneg_tt: rewrite `neg(tt)` with neg_tt (motive `neg(_) = tt`) to reach `⊢ neg(ff) = tt;`, close with `by neg_ff;`. (2) dneg_ff mirrors it: neg_ff (motive `neg(_) = ff`), then `by neg_tt;`. (3) tneg_tt: rewrite the innermost `neg(tt)` with neg_tt (motive `neg(neg(_)) = ff`), then `neg(ff)` with neg_ff (motive `neg(_) = ff`), then `by neg_tt;`.
